Re: Government Code S 87103.5 Income from
Retail Sales Exception to Conflict of Interest
Provisions of the Political Reform Act
Dear Ms. Pritchard:
This letter reguests advice from the Fair Political
Practices Commissiori, pursuant to Government Code S 831-l-4(b).1 I
am requesting informal assistance as a person with a duty to
advise other persons relating to their actions under the
Political Reform Act (2 California Code of Regulations,
S L832e (c) ) .
Facts
A city council candidate owns and controls a weekly
newspaper. A corporation places advertisements for itself,
unrelated to the election, in the newspaper at a cost in excess
of $250. The candidate wins the election and becomes a city
council member. Within 12 months of the advertising purchase, a
matter colnes before the city council which has a material
financial effect on the corporation, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generallY.
rf the ad.vertising purchase renders the corporation a
rsource of incomer to the council member (S 87103 (c) ), then the
council member would have a financial interest in a decision
which has a foreseeable, material financial effect on the
corporation (S 87L03). Because the council member may lot be
invblved in decisions in which he knows he has a financial
interest, he would ordinarily have to disgualify hirnself from the
decision.
1a11 statutory references are to the California Government
Code, unless otherwise sPecified.


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices commission
october 16, 199 O
Page 2
However, if the advertising purchase could fit into the
It income from retail salestr exception (S 871-03.5), then the
corporation would not be a source of income and the counci-I
meml:er would not have to disgualify himself from the decision.
ouestions Presented
l-. 
fs the corporation considered a |tsource of incomerr to the
council member by virtue of the advertising purchase
(5 I71o3 (c) ) ?
2. 
If the answer to Question 1is yes, can the rrincone from
retail salesrt exception be applied so the corporation might
not be a rrsource of incomerr (5 87103.5)?
Di scus s ion
In the Commissionrs Smith letter (I-88-455, L/25/89), lr'}]e
rr income frorn retail salestt exception was applied to a fJ-ower shop
which sold flowers to the public. 
A newspaper sells advertising
space and subscriptions to the public and appears to be a
business entity engaged in retail sa1es. Accordingly, if the
newspaperrs retail, customers constitute a significant segment of
the public generally, and the amount of incorue received by the
newspaper from the corporation is not distinguishable fron the
amount of income recej-ved fron its other retail customers, the
corporationts advertising purchase should not be a source of
income to the council member.
rf you have any questions or require any additional
inforrnation, please feel free to contact me.
Very trul"y yours ,

sections.question:
1. Is the corporation considered a "source of income" to the council member by virtue of the advertising purchase (§ 87103(c))?

2. If the answer to Question 1 is yes, can the "income from retail sales" exception be applied so the corporation might not be a "source of income" (§ 87103.5)?

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a corporation that purchases advertisements in a city council member's newspaper at a cost exceeding $250 constitutes a "source of income" requiring the council member to disqualify himself from decisions affecting that corporation, and whether the "income from retail sales" exception under Government Code § 87103.5 would apply to exclude the newspaper advertising from conflict of interest rules.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney seeking FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. The document includes a discussion section that appears to be the attorney's preliminary analysis rather than FPPC's formal conclusion. No formal CONCLUSION section from FPPC is present. Document references Smith letter (I-88-455) for precedent on retail sales exception.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Is the corporation considered a "source of income" to the council member by virtue of the advertising purchase (§ 87103(c))?

2. If the answer to Question 1 is yes, can the "income from retail sales" exception be applied so the corporation might not be a "source of income" (§ 87103.5)?

embedding.summary: An attorney requests informal FPPC advice regarding whether a corporation purchasing advertising in a newspaper owned by a city council member creates a conflict of interest requiring disqualification, and whether the retail sales exception applies to exclude such advertising income from conflict of interest provisions.
